qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Attacker-controlled Hono tags-split metadata writes files outside the configured output tree
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and package. This finding is a raw-tag path traversal in `packages/hono/src/index.ts` that escapes the output directory during Hono tags-split file emission, while the advisory fixes unescaped `summary` and `operationName` interpolation in `packages/mcp/src/index.ts` for MCP `server.tool(...)` generation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Attacker-controlled schema defaults generate import-time code execution in zod output
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different generator, attacker-controlled field, and sink. This finding emits an unescaped schema `default` into top-level zod output in `packages/zod/src/index.ts` and requires downstream import-time execution, whereas the advisory concerns `@orval/mcp` server generation using unescaped OpenAPI `summary` text in `packages/mcp/src/index.ts`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described `@orval/mcp` code-injection root cause. The advisory patch escapes `verbOption.summary` and `verbOption.operationName` in `packages/mcp/src/index.ts`, while the session's final qualifying findings target a Hono tags-split path traversal and a zod default-value code-injection path.
evidence: The advisory summary and fix commit `80b5fe73b94f120a3a5561952d6d4b0f8d7e928d` describe unescaped OpenAPI `summary` content breaking out of the generated MCP `server.tool(...)` string literal in `packages/mcp/src/index.ts`.

The final qualifying finding `hono_tags_split_path_traversal` is rooted in attacker-controlled tag text becoming an output file path in `packages/hono/src/index.ts`, which is a filesystem containment bug rather than the advisory's MCP string-emission sink.

The final qualifying finding `zod_default_runtime_code_injection` is rooted in attacker-controlled schema `default` values emitted into `packages/zod/src/index.ts`, which is a separate code-generation path from the advisory's MCP `summary` sink.
Session artifacts note an MCP summary-based code-emission hypothesis, but it was not materialized into a final qualifying finding folder in this run.
